---
title: "Dr. Pratik Bhandari"
subtitle: " "
about:
  id: top-heading
  template: marquee 
  image: pratik.png #me33.jpg
  image-width: 18em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    # - icon: bi-mastodon
    #   text: Mastodon
    #   href: "https://neuromatch.social/@pratikb"
    # - icon: bookmark-dash
    #   text: Substack
    #   href: "https://drpratik.substack.com/"
    # - icon: bi-linkedin
    #   text: LinkedIn
    #   href: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/prbhx/"
    - icon: bi-mortarboard-fill
      text: G Scholar
      href: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=de&user=Y0HnEQwAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate"
---
<!-- :::{#intro} -->

<!-- **Cognitive scientist** -->
<!-- &nbsp;&nbsp; -->

**Current research**

I study how the brain/mind processes sound, speech, and language in typical and atypical populations.\
Focusing on underrepresented, non-WEIRD, diverse communities, I aim to expand the scope of cognitive science in resource-constrained settings in Nepal.

> We continuously anticipate what our interlocutor is going to say next. When someone says, “Don’t go out in the rain, else you will get ___,” we predict that the last word will most likely be “wet.” (अचानोमा राखेर कसाइले मासु “काट्छ”।)\
What are the mechanisms involved in making predictions? Are predictions necessary for everyone?

<!-- One of the central questions in my research is how such predictions are made and what resources are involved. -->
> Despite noise in the background, we make sense of what the other person is saying. How effortful are such sense-making processes? What are the cognitive resources involved?

> More broadly, how does understanding the differences in these cognitive processes across typical and atypical populations help us better understand human cognition?

**Trajectory of my research and education**

I am a Research Scientist at the Nepal Center for Cognition and Society ([NCCS](https://nccsnepal.org/)). My work involves cross-cultural cognition research in low-resource settings in Nepal.
I serve as Adjunct Faculty at the National Academy of Medical Sciences in Kathmandu.
I am also a clinical and research consultant at a speech therapy clinic in Kathmandu.

Before returning to Nepal, I received my PhD from Saarland University, Germany (2022/23), working with [Vera Demberg](https://www.uni-saarland.de/lehrstuhl/demberg/members/verademberg.html) and [Jutta Kray](https://www.uni-saarland.de/lehrstuhl/kray/team/jk.html) on the interaction of top-down and bottom-up processes in spoken language comprehension. Previously, I did my Masters in Cognitive Neuroscience of Language at the Basque Center on Cognition, Brain and Language ([BCBL](https://www.bcbl.eu/en)), Spain (2018), where I studied metacognition and bilingual language processing under [David Soto](https://www.bcbl.eu/en/conocenos/equipo/david-soto)'s supervision. \
Prior to that, I got my undergraduate degree (Bachelors in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology) from the Institute of Medicine ([IOM](https://iom.edu.np/)), Tribhuvan University (2015) in Nepal, and worked in [Ramesh Kumar Mishra](https://rameshmishra.squarespace.com/)'s lab at the University of Hyderabad in India (2017).


<!-- [Here](./contents/projects/index.html) is a non-exhaustive list of my research programs,
and [here](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Y0HnEQwAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao) you can find my publications. -->
<!-- A list of my academic publications, presentations, and talks is available [here](https://mfr.de-1.osf.io/render?url=https://osf.io/download/68j3m/?direct%26mode=render). 
And [here](./contents/projects/index.html), you can find some research projects I have carried out. -->
<!-- ::: -->

---

<!--
<u><h1 id="education-section" style="text-align: left; font-size: 22px;">Academic training</h1></u>

**Saarland University**, Germany\
PhD -- Cognitive Science (awarded *magna cum laude*) **|** Nov 2018 – Jan 2023\
Focus: Psycholinguistics & Cognitive Neuroscience

**Basque Center on Cognition, Brain and Language**, Spain\
Master in Cognitive Neuroscience of Language **|** Sep 2017 – Sep 2018\

**Center for Neural and Cognitive Sciences**, University of Hyderabad, India\
Research Intern **|** Dec 2015 – May 2017\

**Institute of Medicine**, Tribhuvan University, Nepal\
Bachelor in Audiology & Speech-Language Pathology **|** Nov 2010 – April 2015


<u><h1 style="text-align: left; font-size: 22px;">Selected publications</h1></u>

**Bhandari, P** (2022). Interaction of top-down and bottom-up processes in spoken language comprehension. [Doctoral dissertation, Saarland University]. *Saarländische Universitäts-und Landesbibliothek*. doi:10.22028/D291-38594. [[html](https://pratik-bhandari.github.io/contents/html-phd/index.html/)]

**Bhandari, P.**, Demberg, V., & Kray, J. (2022). Predictability effects in degraded speech comprehension are reduced as a function of attention. *Language and Cognition, 14*(4), 534-551. [[html](https://doi.org/10.1017/langcog.2022.16)][[pdf](https://mfr.osf.io/render?url=https%3A%2F%2Fosf.io%2Fdownload%2Ftdvhs%2F%3Fdirect%3D%26mode%3Drender)]

**Bhandari, P.**, Prasad, S., & Mishra, R.K. (2020). High proficient bilinguals bring in higher executive control when encountering diverse interlocutors. *Journal of Cultural Cognitive Science. 4*(2), 201-215. [[html](https://doi.org/10.1007/s41809-020-00060-7)] [[pdf](https://mfr.osf.io/render?url=https%3A%2F%2Fosf.io%2Fdownload%2Ffm8da%2F%3Fdirect%3D%26mode%3Drender)]

**Bhandari, P.**, & Khanal, P. (2016). Pride in autistic diversity: against treatment or for inclusion? *The Lancet, 388*(10059), 24477. [[html](https://doi.org/10.1016/S0140-6736(16)32176-6)]

<!-- <p style="font-size: 20px;">Please find all publications and presentations <a href="https://sites.google.com/view/pratik-bhandari/publications">here</a></p> -->

<!-- *** -->
<!-- While you're here, explore my writings in Substack -- [From river to the sea and back](https://drpratik.substack.com/). -->
<!-- <!--literary creations, personal experiences, and scientific musings at "[Scribbles](./contents/literature/index.html)" and "[Blog](./contents/blog/index.html)".--> 


<!-- These are some of my posts: -->

<!-- <!-- 1. [13/03/2023](./contents/blog/posts/2023-03-13_nepal-iq-myth/index.html): The danger and false promise of IQ --> 

<!-- 1. [16/12/2023](./contents/blog/posts/2023-12-16_movie-primer/index.html): 	Identity enigma -->

<!-- 1. [14/08/2023](./contents/blog/posts/2023-08-14_live-n-leave/index.html): Reflections on farewells and returns -->

<!-- 2. [21/01/2023](./contents/blog/posts/2023-01-21_phd-afterthought/index.html): PhD defence: An afterthought -->

<!-- 3. [08/08/2022](./contents/blog/posts/2022-08-08_speech-rate/index.html): Speaking fast and slow -->

<!-- 4. [25/07/2022](./contents/blog/posts/2022-07-25_attention-prediction/index.html): Attention is mostly what you need -->

<!-- 5. [26/10/2021](./contents/literature/posts/2021-10-26-operation-room/index.html): In the operating room -->

<!-- *** -->
<!-- <a rel="me" href="https://neuromatch.social/@pratikb"></a> -->

<!-- <div -->
<!-- <b style="text-align:center;">Subscribe to my newsletter for updates.</b> -->
<!-- </div> -->

<!-- <iframe -->
<!-- src="https://drpratik.substack.com/embed" width="800" height="150" style="border:1px solid #EEE; background:white;" frameborder="0" scrolling="no"> -->
<!-- </iframe> -->



<!-- E-mail me <a href="mailto:work@pratikb.com"><span class="bi bi-envelope-at-fill"></span></a>, or find me in [other places](./contents/contact.html) 🙂  -->
